title: Smooth Brazilian Rice Bowl
description: A roasted, creamy low-fat bowl that blends muscovado sugar, wok-hei soy sauce, and fresh veggies into a light Brazilian-inspired meal.
instructions: Rinse 1 cup jasmine rice until water runs clear and add 1.5 cups water to a saucepan; bring to a boil, then reduce heat, cover, and simmer 15 minutes until water is absorbed, fluff with a fork.

While the rice cooks, preheat oven to 200°C and season 200g diced chicken breast with salt, pepper, 1 tbsp muscovado sugar, 1 tbsp wok-hei soy sauce, and 1 tsp grated ginger; roast for 20 minutes until the internal temperature reaches 74°C.
Steam 1 cup broccoli florets for 5 minutes until bright green and tender.
In a small bowl whisk together 1 tbsp wok-hei soy sauce, 1 tbsp muscovado sugar, 1 tbsp lime juice, 1 tsp grated ginger, and 2 tbsp low-fat plain Greek yogurt until smooth.
Dice 1/2 cup avocado and set aside.
When the chicken is done, allow it to rest for 5 minutes, then shred it with two forks.
Assemble the bowl by placing fluffy rice at the bottom, topping with shredded roasted chicken, steamed broccoli, diced avocado, and a drizzle of the creamy sauce.
Sprinkle toasted sesame seeds and a pinch of chili flakes over the bowl for crunch and heat.
Serve immediately and enjoy the smooth, creamy, low-fat Brazilian rice bowl.
